untranslatable_reason: This signal is fundamentally qualitative and forward-looking, requiring external event detection and probabilistic assessment of future catalysts (4-9 months ahead). The rule depends on: (1) identification of specific structural changes/events not observable in price/technical data, (2) subjective probability assessment of outcomes based on policy direction and electoral momentum, and (3) timing of 'thesis crystallization' which cannot be encoded in historical OHLCV indicators. Technical indicators cannot detect or forecast specific catalysts, policy shifts, or electoral momentum. This requires real-time news/fundamental analysis and manual thesis assessment outside the scope of a purely technical backtest engine.
translation_notes: SCHWAGER_DRY_0_2 is a catalyst-driven, discretionary entry strategy. The entry conditions reference forward-looking structural analysis ('catalyst event identified 4-9 months ahead') and subjective confidence thresholds ('55-60% based on structural/leading indicators') that are fundamentally incompatible with historical technical analysis. The exit condition ('exit if conviction drops below 50%') also requires continuous reassessment of fundamental conviction rather than technical triggers. To implement this signal, a trader would need: (1) an external event/news feed, (2) a fundamental/macro analysis framework, (3) manual thesis monitoring, and (4) position sizing logic outside technical rules. No combination of price, volume, or technical indicators can replicate catalyst detection or fundamental conviction assessment.
